title: Buying Compound (COMP): Safest Exchanges and Steps
content: For participants looking to engage with the Compound governance process, acquiring COMP is the first step. Compound provides essential lending infrastructure, and the token allows holders to vote on protocol improvements. | Metric | Details | | :--- | :--- | | Current Price | {{LIVE_PRICE}} | | Market Cap | {{LIVE_MARKET_CAP}} | | Market Rank | {{LIVE_RANK}} | | 24H Change | {{LIVE_24H_CHANGE}} | | Date | {{LIVE_DATE}} | ## Why Buy Compound? Compound stands alongside industry leaders like Uniswap and Chainlink as a foundational piece of the DeFi stack. Owning COMP provides a voice in the future of the lending protocol, though it should be viewed through the lens of a high-risk asset. ## Top Exchange Options Most major centralized exchanges support COMP due to its longevity and presence in the Ethereum ecosystem. Beginners often start with regulated platforms, while advanced users might utilize decentralized exchanges to maintain custody during the transaction. ## Purchase Tutorial 1. Choose a secure, reputable exchange. 2. Complete the required identity verification (KYC). 3. Deposit fiat currency (USD, EUR, etc.) via bank transfer or card. 4. Navigate to the COMP trading pair (e.g., COMP/USDT). 5. Place your order and confirm the transaction. ## Securing Your Tokens After purchasing, transferring tokens to a hardware wallet is the industry standard for security. Storing tokens on an exchange introduces third-party risk. By holding your own keys, you retain full control over your governance participation. ## Important Risks As noted by our TokenRadar Risk Score of 7, Compound is a high-risk investment. Governance tokens are susceptible to market volatility, and users should only allocate funds they can afford to lose. Always monitor the protocol's development status to stay informed. --- *Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice. Always do your own research (DYOR).*
